description: Time access and conversions. Provides functions for working with time values, formatting, parsing, and sleeping.

functions.name: time

returns.description: The current time in seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC)

functions.description: Returns the time as a floating-point number expressed in seconds since the epoch. Note that even though the time is always returned as a floating-point number, not all systems provide time with a better precision than 1 second.

functions.name: clock

returns.description: CPU time in seconds as a floating-point number

functions.description: Returns the current processor time as a floating-point number expressed in seconds. The precision depends on the platform. Use this for benchmarking Python code or timing algorithms.

functions.name: sleep

params.name: secs
params.description: Number of seconds to suspend execution. Can be a float for sub-second precision.

functions.description: Suspend execution of the current thread for the given number of seconds. The argument may be a floating-point number for sub-second sleep times. The actual suspension time may be less due to signals or more due to scheduling.

functions.name: gmtime

params.name: secs
params.description: Seconds since the epoch. If not provided or None, the current time as returned by time() is used.

functions.description: Converts a time expressed in seconds since the epoch to a struct_time in UTC. If secs is not provided, the current time as returned by time() is used. Fields in the result have no daylight saving adjustment.

functions.name: localtime

params.name: secs
params.description: Seconds since the epoch. If not provided or None, the current time as returned by time() is used.

returns.description: A time.struct_time object in local time

functions.description: Converts a time expressed in seconds since the epoch to a struct_time in local time. If secs is not provided, the current time as returned by time() is used. The DST flag is set if DST is in effect for the given time.

functions.name: mktime

params.name: t
params.description: A struct_time or 9-tuple expressing local time (not UTC)

returns.description: Seconds since the epoch as a floating-point number

functions.description: Converts a struct_time or 9-tuple expressing local time to seconds since the epoch. This is the inverse of localtime(). The tm_isdst field of the input may be set to 1 for DST, 0 for non-DST, or -1 to let mktime determine DST.

functions.name: asctime

params.name: t
params.description: A struct_time or 9-tuple. If not provided, the current local time is used.

returns.description: A 24-character string of the form 'Sun Jun 20 23:21:05 1993'

functions.description: Converts a struct_time or tuple representing time to a 24-character string of the form 'Sun Jun 20 23:21:05 1993'. If no argument is provided, the current local time is used.

functions.name: ctime

params.name: secs
params.description: Seconds since the epoch. If not provided or None, the current time is used.

functions.description: Converts a time expressed in seconds since the epoch to a string representing local time. Equivalent to asctime(localtime(secs)). If secs is not provided, the current time is used.

functions.name: strftime

params.name: format
params.description: Format string using directives such as %Y (year), %m (month), %d (day), %H (hour), %M (minute), %S (second), etc.

params.name: t
params.description: A struct_time or 9-tuple. If not provided, the current local time is used.

functions.description: Converts a struct_time or tuple representing time to a string as specified by the format argument. Common directives: %Y=4-digit year, %m=month (01-12), %d=day (01-31), %H=hour (00-23), %M=minute (00-59), %S=second (00-61).

functions.name: strptime

params.name: format
params.description: Format string using the same directives as strftime()

returns.description: A struct_time parsed from the string

functions.description: Parses a string representing a time according to a format. The return value is a struct_time. The format parameter uses the same directives as strftime(). Raises ValueError if the string does not match the format.

classes.name: struct_time
classes.description: Named tuple returned by gmtime(), localtime(), and strptime(). It holds a sequence of 9 integers: year, month, day, hour, minute, second, weekday, Julian day, and DST flag. Values can be accessed by index or by attribute name.

params.name: tuple
params.description: A 9-element tuple: (tm_year, tm_mon, tm_mday, tm_hour, tm_min, tm_sec, tm_wday, tm_yday, tm_isdst)

constructors.description: Creates a struct_time from a 9-element tuple.

fields.name: tm_sec
fields.description: Second, range 0-61 (60 and 61 for leap seconds)

fields.name: tm_wday
fields.description: Day of the week, range 0-6 (Monday is 0)

constants.name: timezone
constants.description: The offset of the local (non-DST) timezone, in seconds west of UTC. Negative in most of Western Europe, positive in the US, zero in the UK.

constants.name: altzone
constants.description: The offset of the local DST timezone, in seconds west of UTC. Defined only if the local timezone has DST.

constants.name: daylight
constants.description: Nonzero if a DST timezone is defined for the local area.

constants.name: tzname
constants.description: A tuple of two strings: the name of the local non-DST timezone and the name of the local DST timezone.
